---
title: "Advanced hooks: useBlocker, useOutletContext, useAsyncValue, useAsyncError"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Advanced hooks: useBlocker, useOutletContext, useAsyncValue, useAsyncError}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

## Overview

This vignette covers four hooks that address specific routing patterns beyond
basic data loading and navigation:

| Hook | What it does |
|---|---|
| `useBlocker()` | Intercept navigation — e.g. warn about unsaved changes |
| `useOutletContext()` | Read data passed from a parent route through `Outlet(context = ...)` |

They work inside data routers (`RouterProvider()`, `createHashRouter()`,
`createMemoryRouter()`).

---

## `useBlocker()`

`useBlocker()` intercepts navigation when a JavaScript condition returns
`true`. It exposes a `state` field:

| State | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `"unblocked"` | No navigation is being intercepted (default) |
| `"blocked"` | A navigation was intercepted — show a confirmation UI |
| `"proceeding"` | User confirmed; navigation is in progress |

### Arguments

| Argument | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `into` | yes | Component that receives the value |
| `as` | no | Prop to inject into. Defaults to `"children"` |
| `selector` | no | Field from the blocker object. Defaults to `"state"` |
| `shouldBlock` | no | A `JS()` function receiving `{ currentLocation, nextLocation, historyAction }` and returning `true` to block or `false` to allow. Defaults to `FALSE` (never blocks). |

### Show blocker state

```r
useBlocker(
  tags$span(),
  shouldBlock = JS(
    "({ currentLocation, nextLocation }) =>
      currentLocation.pathname !== nextLocation.pathname"
  )
)
# renders "unblocked", "blocked", or "proceeding"
```

### Full example

The companion components `BlockerProceedButton()` and `BlockerResetButton()`
let the user confirm or cancel the blocked navigation:

```r
library(reactRouter)
library(htmltools)

should_block <- JS(
  "({ currentLocation, nextLocation }) =>
    currentLocation.pathname !== nextLocation.pathname"
)

RouterProvider(
  Route(
    path = "/",
    element = div(
      NavLink(to = "/",      "Home"),
      NavLink(to = "/other", "Other page"),
      Outlet()
    ),
    Route(
      index = TRUE,
      element = div(
        tags$h3("Home — blocker active"),
        tags$p("Blocker state: ", useBlocker(tags$code(), shouldBlock = should_block)),
        # Shown when state = "blocked":
        tags$p("Leave without saving?"),
        BlockerProceedButton("Leave",  shouldBlock = should_block),
        BlockerResetButton( "Stay",    shouldBlock = should_block)
      )
    ),
    Route(
      path = "other",
      element = div(tags$h3("Other page"))
    )
  )
)
```

`shouldBlock` must be the **same JS function object** passed to all three —
`useBlocker`, `BlockerProceedButton`, and `BlockerResetButton` — so they all
reference the same blocker instance.

### Conditional blocking

Pass a condition that depends on form state embedded in the JS:

```r
# Block only when a specific query param indicates a dirty form
JS("({ nextLocation }) => nextLocation.pathname === '/confirm'")

# Always block (useful for testing)
JS("() => true")

# Never block
FALSE  # the default
```

---

## `useOutletContext()`

Parent routes can pass data to child routes through `Outlet(context = ...)`.
Any child route reads that data with `useOutletContext()`, avoiding prop-drilling
through deeply nested layouts.

### Arguments

| Argument | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `into` | yes | Component that receives the value |
| `as` | no | Prop to inject into. Defaults to `"children"` |
| `selector` | no | Field to extract from the context object. `NULL` (default) injects the full context as JSON |

### Passing context from a parent route

Add a `context` argument to `Outlet()`:

```r
Route(
  path = "/",
  element = div(
    # Pass a named list — any R object that serialises to JSON
    Outlet(context = list(user = "Alice", role = "admin")),
    NavLink(to = "/profile", "Profile")
  ),
  Route(
    path = "profile",
    element = div(
      tags$p("Name: ",  useOutletContext(tags$span(), selector = "user")),
      tags$p("Role: ",  useOutletContext(tags$span(), selector = "role"))
    )
  )
)
```

### Full example

```r
library(reactRouter)
library(htmltools)

user <- list(name = "Alice", role = "admin", email = "alice@example.com")

RouterProvider(
  Route(
    path = "/",
    element = div(
      NavLink(to = "/",        "Dashboard"),
      NavLink(to = "/profile", "Profile"),
      Outlet(context = user)
    ),
    Route(
      index = TRUE,
      element = div(
        tags$p("Welcome, ",
               useOutletContext(tags$strong(), selector = "name"))
      )
    ),
    Route(
      path = "profile",
      element = div(
        tags$p("Name: ",  useOutletContext(tags$span(), selector = "name")),
        tags$p("Email: ", useOutletContext(tags$span(), selector = "email")),
        tags$p("Role: ",  useOutletContext(tags$span(), selector = "role"))
      )
    )
  )
)
```

The context is passed once at the layout level and is available to every
child route without repeating it in each `element`.
